canonical_definition_en: A stable unit treated as a node in a categorical or design model
A structure respecting map between objects or modeled states
The operation that connects compatible morphisms into one path
A morphism that leaves an object unchanged under composition
A structured picture that states objects morphisms and their relations
Describes a diagram where alternative paths preserve the same meaning
A map between categories that preserves objects morphisms and composition
A coherent mapping between functors over the same source category
A consistency condition stating that corresponding paths preserve the same meaning under a change of view
A paired relationship between functors that captures a best fit translation
A definition that characterizes a construction by its unique mapping behavior
A construction that combines required components into one canonical object from which each component can be recovered
A construction that makes alternatives explicit by giving each variant a named entry into one shared boundary
A category equipped with a tensor like way to compose things in parallel
A graphical notation for sequential and parallel composition
A structure that packages effectful computation behind explicit composition rules
A form of composition specialized for effectful computations
A collection of objects and morphisms with associative composition and identities
A construction for joining structures under a shared constraint
A construction for merging structures along a shared boundary
A law stating that grouping does not change the result of composition
A property that must remain true across transformations or execution steps
A claim that two structures produce the same observable behavior
A precise statement of required behavior constraints and assumptions
A documented method for checking that claims and constraints hold
The concrete records that justify acceptance of a deliverable

term_ja: トレーサビリティ行列
canonical_definition_en: A table that links requirements design choices tests and evidence
A condition that limits allowed behavior to reduce risk
Recorded origin and transformation history of an artifact or datum
A diagram that encodes a preserved result across multiple paths

term_ja: インターフェース契約
canonical_definition_en: A precise statement of inputs outputs guarantees and obligations at a boundary
A transformation defined only for inputs that satisfy stated preconditions or domain restrictions
notes: Useful for workflow boundaries and review contracts

canonical_definition_en: A transformation defined for every input in its stated domain
notes: Useful when deciding whether a workflow step is universally applicable

canonical_definition_en: A durable document or model used to explain review and govern a system
The authoritative representation used to align multiple design views

term_ja: アーキテクチャモデル
canonical_definition_en: A structural description of components boundaries and interactions
notes: Used in TOC and case study

canonical_definition_en: A representation of the system during execution including operational states transitions and evidence obligations
A mismatch where a translated artifact no longer preserves the intended meaning of its source view
A reviewer-facing representation that preserves decision-relevant meaning while omitting operational detail that does not affect judgment
A defined translation between data structures or interface schemas
A staged plan for replacing or restructuring a running system
A component that reshapes one interface into another compatible form
A simplified interface that hides internal complexity behind one boundary
A condition where linked views or artifacts evolve at different times and temporarily disagree
A mismatch in which supposedly corresponding paths or views no longer preserve the same design meaning

term_ja: レビューチェックポイント
canonical_definition_en: A planned decision point where artifacts and risks are examined
A controlled return to a previous safe operational state
A sequence of runtime or human activities that delivers system outcomes
A delegation pattern that limits agent authority by task scope and policy
A software actor that plans or acts using model driven reasoning and tools

term_ja: エージェント型ワークフロー
canonical_definition_en: A workflow that includes agent planning action and handoff steps
notes: Used in the title and TOC

canonical_definition_en: An invocation of an external capability by an agent or orchestrator
A mandatory human approval step before higher risk actions continue

term_ja: エスカレーション方針
canonical_definition_en: A rule that routes uncertain or risky cases to a higher authority
A recorded sequence of steps inputs outputs and decisions during execution

term_ja: オーケストレーション
canonical_definition_en: The coordination of multiple effectful steps tools or agents into one flow
A boundary that makes side effects explicit and reviewable
A durable record of actions decisions and changes for later inspection
The task state instructions and references supplied to a model at invocation time
A design approach that reasons about systems through explicit artifacts boundaries and composable transformations
A documented boundary that assigns decision rights obligations and escalation rules across people tools and systems
A requested repository or system modification that enters the governed review flow
A bounded plan that states checks scope and reviewer expectations before execution
A change artifact that has passed required policy checks and human approval
A rule-driven checkpoint that blocks progress until stated policy conditions hold
notes: Used in the running example and Chapter 01
